previewItems.biography:
Helping parents guide their athlete to college success. 
Former D1 Coach, Scout, & Recruiter
Get your playbook for your exact sport & year.👇

externalUrls.title: Personal guidance for your athlete’s sport & year.

latestPosts.caption:
There are a lot of new faces here, so I wanted to properly introduce myself.

Before I ever coached, scouted, or recruited at the Division I level, I was an athlete trying to figure out recruiting too.

My family and I were trying to get seen, build the right profile, make the right decisions, and understand what actually mattered.

Eventually, we got help from someone who understood the process, and that changed everything.

Years later, after coaching, scouting, and recruiting at the college level, I saw the other side too.

I saw how coaches evaluate athletes.

I saw how fit matters.

I saw how timing, communication, and clarity can change the entire process.

That is why this page exists.

To help families feel more informed, more prepared, and less alone while navigating college recruiting.

If you’re new here, drop a 👋 in the comments.

If you have a recruiting question, drop it below too. I’ll try to answer as many as I can.

#CollegeRecruiting #SportsParents #StudentAthlete #RecruitingTips #PremierAthletes #RecruitNation

There are five legitimate ways to play college sports: NCAA D1, D2, D3, NAIA, and NJCAA. Each offers different scholarships, competition levels, time commitments, and college experiences.

Chasing the wrong level costs you time, money, and opportunities. Understanding the full landscape? That changes everything.

Comment MAP and I'll send you my free 12-question recruiting assessment — see exactly where your athlete stands and which path makes the most sense for your family.

#CollegeRecruiting #StudentAthlete #CollegeSports #AthleticsScholarships #RecruitingProcess #RecruitingWithAlex #PremierAthletes

When a college coach calls — don’t panic.
Here’s what to say, and how to make a great impression 👇

As a former D1 coach who’s made hundreds of these calls, there’s nothing worse than losing momentum because a player isn’t ready.

Here’s what to expect (and how to prepare):

🎯 What they’ll ask you:
1️⃣ “How’s your season going?” → Start with the team, then your best stats.
2️⃣ “How are your grades?” → Know your GPA — keep them strong.
3️⃣ “Have you thought about a major?” → Show you’ve done your homework (“I’m thinking business, communication, or finance”).

🎯 What YOU should ask them:
1️⃣ “What’s a day in the life like for your players?”
2️⃣ “What are you looking for at my position?”
3️⃣ “How does your program focus on player development — athletically, in the weight room, and academically?”

These are the conversations that separate serious recruits from everyone else.

Comment MAP below and I’ll send you the 12-question quiz to see if your athlete’s mindset and habits match what college coaches look for.

#CollegeRecruiting #PremierAthletes #StudentAthlete #ParentsOfAthletes #AllSportsRecruiting #RecruitingTips #CollegeSports #CollegeAthleteJourney #HighSchoolAthlete #D1Recruiting #Whenacollegecoachcalls

Championships aren't built on motivation.

They're built on standards.

The habits you choose every day eventually become your results. That applies to training, academics, and the recruiting process.

Small standards today create bigger opportunities tomorrow.

Share this with a parent or athlete who believes consistency beats shortcuts.

#Leadership #CollegeRecruiting #StudentAthlete #SportsParents #PremierAthletes

Most athletes overcomplicate their first email. 

Most coaches want the opposite. 

They want enough information to quickly determine whether your athlete fits what they're recruiting. 

Keep it simple.
Make it easy to evaluate.
Make it easy to respond. 

Because recruiting isn't about sending the perfect email. 

It's about creating opportunities and building relationships with coaches. 

That's just one of the many topics we cover inside the College Recruiting Playbook. 

Comment PLAN and we'll send you information about the College Recruiting Playbook built specifically for your athlete's sport and graduation year. 

#CollegeRecruiting #StudentAthlete #SportsParents #RecruitingTips #PremierAthletes

Belief comes before achievement.

Every great athlete starts by believing they're capable of something bigger before anyone else does.

Confidence isn't pretending you'll succeed. 

It's choosing to keep moving forward even when the outcome isn't guaranteed.

Save this for the days your athlete needs the reminder.

#Mindset #CollegeRecruiting #StudentAthlete #SportsParents #PremierAthletes

Most recruiting emails never get a response.

Not because the athlete isn't good enough.

Because the email gave the coach no reason to keep reading.

College coaches receive hundreds of recruiting emails every week. They don't have time to read long introductions or generic messages that could've been sent to every school in the country.

The emails that stand out are simple.

They tell the coach exactly who the athlete is, include the key information they need, and make it clear why that specific program is a good fit.

And one more thing...

The email should always come from the athlete.

Authenticity matters. Coaches can tell when someone else wrote it.

Inside the College Recruiting Playbook, I walk you through exactly how to write recruiting emails, what to include, when to send them, and how to follow up after camps and showcases.

Because getting noticed isn't about sending more emails.
It's about sending better ones.

Comment PLAN and I'll send you the Playbook.

The best player doesn’t always get the offer.

After 11 years as a Division I coach and recruiter, I can tell you this:

I’ve seen athletes with incredible talent get overlooked.
And I’ve seen less talented athletes earn the offer.

The difference usually isn’t talent.

It’s visibility.
It’s communication.
It’s having a recruiting plan.

College coaches can’t recruit an athlete they don’t know exists.

The families who succeed aren’t always the ones with the most gifted athlete.
They’re the ones who understand how recruiting actually works and take consistent action.

That’s exactly why we created the Division Fit Finder Quiz.

It shows where your athlete stands based on the same categories college coaches evaluate, so you can stop guessing and start making smarter recruiting decisions.

Comment FIT and I’ll send you the link.

TCU offers something many families are looking for:

Strong academics.

Big-time athletics.

A vibrant campus experience.

And opportunities that extend far beyond graduation.

When evaluating schools, don't just ask:

"Can my athlete play there?"

Also ask:

"Can they thrive there?"

The best recruiting decisions balance athletics, academics, culture, and long-term opportunity.

Save this post and share it with another recruiting family.

#TCU #CollegeRecruiting #StudentAthlete #PremierAthletes #RecruitNation

Talent can get you noticed.

Teamwork, trust, and doing your job make great teams.

The same is true in recruiting. The families who communicate well, stay organized, and work together throughout the process usually create the most opportunities.

#CollegeRecruiting #Teamwork #StudentAthlete #SportsParents #PremierAthletes

Most families hear “D3” and immediately think “lesser opportunity.”

That is a mistake.

Some of the best college fits in the country are sitting at the Division 3 level.

Strong academics.
Competitive athletics.
Great campuses.
Real player development.
And a college experience that actually makes sense for the athlete.

The problem is too many families are chasing the logo instead of chasing the right opportunity.

D1 is not the goal.

The goal is finding a school where your athlete can play, develop, graduate, and actually enjoy the next four years.

Here are 5 underrated D3 schools more high school athletes should know:

5. University of Redlands — California

4) Berry College — Georgia

3. Endicott College — Massachusetts

2) Salisbury University — Maryland

1. Trinity University — Texas

Do not overlook a school just because it does not have the division label you thought you wanted.

The right fit beats the wrong level every single time.

If you want guidance on how to start gaining traction with schools like this, comment “PLAY” and I’ll send you more information.
